name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help for oh-my-posh

name: --config
-c
options.description: The config file to use

name: --print
-p
options.description: Print the init script

name: --config
-c
options.description: The config file to use

name: --config
-c
options.description: The config file to use

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Edit the config file, example usage: 'oh-my-posh config edit'

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Migrate the config file, example usage: 'oh-my-posh config migrate --config ~/.config.omp.json'

name: --write
-w
options.description: Write the migrated config to the configuration file, example usage: 'oh-my-posh config migrate --write'

name: --format
-f
options.description: The file format to use

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export the config file, example usage: 'oh-my-posh config export --config ~/.config.omp.toml'

name: --output
-o
options.description: The file to output the migrated config to, example usage: 'oh-my-posh config export --output ~/.config.omp.json'

name: --format
-f
options.description: The file format to use

name: --config
-c
options.description: The config file to use

name: --terminal-width
-w
options.description: The terminal width, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --terminal-width 200'

options.name: --command
options.description: The current command for tooltips, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print tooltip --command az'

name: --error
-e
options.description: The last exit code, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --error 127'

options.name: --eval
options.description: Use eval to render the prompt, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --eval'

options.name: --execution-time
options.description: Use last command execution time in millis, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --execution-time 200'

name: --plain
-p
options.description: Print a prompt without ASNI codes, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --plain'

options.name: --pswd
options.description: The current working directory according to PowerShell, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --pswd C:\Users\user\Documents'

args.name: POWERSHELL WORKING DIRECTORY
args.description: The working directory according to PowerShell

options.name: --pwd
options.description: The current working directory, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --pwd /home/user/Documents'

options.name: --shell
options.description: The current shell, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --shell fish'

options.name: --stack-count
options.description: The directory stack count, example usage: 'oh-my-posh print primary --stack-count 1'

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Display the oh-my-posh version, example usage: 'oh-my-posh version'
